channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: General Fellowship Programme 2027–2028 (Residential Research Fellowship)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the General Fellowship Programme 2027–2028 hosted by the Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study in Uppsala, Sweden. This prestigious fellowship supports outstanding scholars in the humanities and social sciences by providing time and space to pursue advanced research in an international and interdisciplinary academic environment. The programme welcomes scholars from universities […]

The post General Fellowship Programme 2027–2028 (Residential Research Fellowship) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the General Fellowship Programme 2027–2028 hosted by the Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study in Uppsala, Sweden.

This prestigious fellowship supports outstanding scholars in the humanities and social sciences by providing time and space to pursue advanced research in an international and interdisciplinary academic environment.

The programme welcomes scholars from universities and research institutions around the world to join a vibrant community of researchers at the collegium.

About the Fellowship

The General Fellowship Programme is the core fellowship offered by the Swedish Collegium for Advanced Study. It is a residential research fellowship that allows scholars to focus on independent research while engaging with other international fellows through seminars, workshops, and academic discussions.

The collegium aims to foster innovative research and interdisciplinary collaboration, bringing together scholars working on diverse topics within the humanities and social sciences.

Fellowship Features

Selected fellows benefit from:

🎓 Time to focus on independent research
🌍 Participation in an international academic community
🏛 A residential fellowship environment in Uppsala
📚 Access to seminars, workshops, and collaborative networks

The programme encourages scholars from diverse backgrounds and institutions to apply.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD (or equivalent doctoral degree)

  • Have obtained their PhD at least three years before the application

  • Demonstrate a strong academic track record, including significant publications

  • Be actively engaged in international research networks

Both early-career and senior scholars are welcome to apply.

Who Should Apply

The fellowship is ideal for researchers working in:

  • Humanities

  • Social sciences

  • Interdisciplinary research areas related to these fields

Applicants should have a clear research project they plan to pursue during the fellowship period.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the official fellowship portal.

For more Fellowship Opportunities from OFY, Click Here.

The post General Fellowship Programme 2027–2028 (Residential Research Fellowship) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Climate Collaborative Postgraduate Fellowships 2026 – Study at the University of Ghana (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Africa Climate Collaborative Postgraduate Fellowships, an exciting opportunity for emerging climate scholars across West Africa. The fellowship is hosted by University of Ghana through the Africa Climate Collaborative project, in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation. The program aims to support Master’s, PhD, and Postdoctoral researchers who are passionate about […]

The post Africa Climate Collaborative Postgraduate Fellowships 2026 – Study at the University of Ghana (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Africa Climate Collaborative Postgraduate Fellowships, an exciting opportunity for emerging climate scholars across West Africa.

The fellowship is hosted by University of Ghana through the Africa Climate Collaborative project, in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation.

The program aims to support Master’s, PhD, and Postdoctoral researchers who are passionate about climate resilience, sustainability, and driving impactful research in Africa.

About the Fellowship

The Africa Climate Collaborative Fellowship seeks to build a new generation of climate resilience leaders and sustainability researchers in West Africa.

The program promotes interdisciplinary research, collaboration with experts, and policy-relevant solutions to address climate challenges across the continent.

Successful fellows will benefit from a strong academic and research environment, mentorship from experienced faculty, and access to regional and international research networks.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a comprehensive support package, including:

🎓 Full tuition coverage at the University of Ghana
🏠 Accommodation support
💰 Monthly stipend
🔬 Research funding and academic resources
👩‍🏫 Structured mentorship from faculty and research leaders
🌍 Opportunities for collaborative research with national and international partners

Who Should Apply

The fellowship is designed for ambitious scholars across West Africa who are committed to contributing to climate resilience and sustainability solutions.

The program strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women

  • Persons with disabilities

  • Refugees

  • Internally displaced persons

The initiative is committed to building an inclusive and diverse academic community.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old as of August 1, 2026

  • Be applying for Master’s, PhD, or Postdoctoral research

  • Be from a West African country

  • Demonstrate strong interest in climate resilience, sustainability, or climate policy

Application Deadline

📅 May 31, 2026

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official portal.

For more Scholarship opportunities from OFY, visit Here.

The post Africa Climate Collaborative Postgraduate Fellowships 2026 – Study at the University of Ghana (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme 2026: Up to £10,000 Grants for Community Summer Projects (Apply by March 31, 2026)
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Community organizations working to create meaningful summer activities for adults now have an opportunity to access funding through the Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme 2026. The program is delivered by Groundwork and provides grants to support community-based initiatives that engage adults aged 21 to 65 through summer activities. With funding of up to £10,000 […]

The post Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme 2026: Up to £10,000 Grants for Community Summer Projects (Apply by March 31, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Community organizations working to create meaningful summer activities for adults now have an opportunity to access funding through the Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme 2026.

The program is delivered by Groundwork and provides grants to support community-based initiatives that engage adults aged 21 to 65 through summer activities.

With funding of up to £10,000 per project, the initiative aims to support local organizations that foster social connection, wellbeing, and community engagement.

About the Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme

The Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme focuses on supporting community-led projects that bring people together during the summer months.

Many adults face social isolation, limited recreational opportunities, or barriers to community participation. This program helps organizations design activities that encourage connection, wellbeing, and positive community engagement.

Projects should focus on creating inclusive and accessible opportunities for adults between the ages of 21 and 65.

Funding Details

Successful applicants can receive funding to support the planning and delivery of summer community activities.

Key details include:

  • Grant amount: Up to £10,000

  • Funding type: Community project grant

  • Focus: Summer activities for adults aged 21–65

Funding may be used to support project implementation, materials, community events, or other eligible program costs.

What Types of Projects Are Encouraged?

Projects may include activities that:

  • Promote community engagement and participation

  • Encourage social interaction and wellbeing

  • Provide educational or recreational summer programs

  • Support local community development

Organizations are encouraged to design creative activities that address the needs of their communities.

Who Should Apply?

The grant is intended for organizations that are actively working within communities and are able to deliver summer programs that engage adults.

Applicants should demonstrate how their project will benefit participants and strengthen community connections.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by Tuesday, March 31, 2026. Late submissions will not be considered.

How to Apply

Learn more about the eligibility criteria and submit your application through the official page.

For more Grant opportunities from OFY, Click Here.

The post Rowntrees Free the Fund Community Programme 2026: Up to £10,000 Grants for Community Summer Projects (Apply by March 31, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 – Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunity for International Students
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 is one of the most competitive and prestigious fully funded graduate research scholarships available in Australia. Funded by the Australian Government and administered by Monash University, this scholarship supports outstanding domestic and international students pursuing Master’s by Research or Doctoral (PhD) degrees. The RTP Scholarship is designed […]

The post Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 – Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 is one of the most competitive and prestigious fully funded graduate research scholarships available in Australia. Funded by the Australian Government and administered by Monash University, this scholarship supports outstanding domestic and international students pursuing Master’s by Research or Doctoral (PhD) degrees.

The RTP Scholarship is designed to attract high-performing students with strong academic backgrounds and significant research potential. Through this program, scholars gain the opportunity to conduct advanced research, collaborate with world-class academics, and contribute to groundbreaking discoveries in multiple fields.

This scholarship not only provides comprehensive financial support but also enables scholars to focus entirely on their research without financial stress. By covering tuition costs and offering a generous living allowance, the program helps talented researchers from around the world build their academic and professional careers in Australia.

Overview of the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026

The Research Training Program (RTP) is a government-funded initiative in Australia that supports postgraduate research training at Australian universities. The scholarship is awarded to students enrolled in graduate research degrees, including PhD programs and research-based Master’s degrees.

The program at Monash University aims to develop the next generation of global researchers by providing funding, mentorship, and access to state-of-the-art research facilities.

The scholarship is highly competitive, with selections based on academic excellence, research achievements, and the applicant’s potential to contribute to the global research community.

Financial Benefits of the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026

The Monash RTP Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity that provides significant financial support to recipients throughout their research studies.

1. Annual Living Allowance (Stipend)

Successful applicants receive a living stipend of approximately AUD $37,145 per year (2026 rate) to cover living expenses while studying in Australia.

2. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

The RTP includes a tuition fee offset, meaning the scholarship covers the cost of tuition for the entire duration of the research program.

3. Relocation Allowance

Eligible international students may receive financial assistance to help cover relocation expenses when moving to Australia to begin their studies.

4. Additional Scholarship Packaging

In some cases, the RTP stipend may be combined with other Monash-funded scholarships to enhance the financial support package for international students.

5. Access to Research Facilities

Recipients gain access to world-class laboratories, academic mentors, and professional development opportunities within Monash University.

Duration of the Scholarship

The duration of the Monash RTP Scholarship depends on the level of study:

  • Doctoral (PhD) Programs: Up to 3.5 years

  • Master’s by Research Programs: Typically 2 years

Students are expected to remain enrolled as full-time research candidates throughout the scholarship period.

Eligible Fields of Study

The Monash RTP Scholarship supports research across all academic disciplines offered by Monash University.

Examples of research areas include:

  • Engineering and Technology

  • Medical and Health Sciences

  • Science and Biotechnology

  • Business and Economics

  • Education

  • Social Sciences

  • Information Technology

  • Environmental Science

  • Law and Public Policy

Because the scholarship covers all research fields, students from diverse academic backgrounds can apply.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Countries and Regions)

One of the major advantages of this scholarship is its global accessibility.

Eligible Nationalities

Applicants can come from:

  • All countries worldwide

  • Domestic applicants in Australia

  • International students from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and the Middle East

Specifically, the scholarship is open to:

  • Australian citizens

  • New Zealand citizens

  • Australian permanent residents

  • Australian humanitarian visa holders

  • International students from any country

This makes the RTP Scholarship an excellent opportunity for students from developing countries seeking high-quality research training abroad.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet several academic and institutional requirements.

1. Academic Excellence

Applicants must demonstrate outstanding academic performance in their previous degrees.

2. Research Potential

Candidates should have strong research capabilities, often demonstrated through:

  • Research projects

  • Publications

  • Academic achievements

  • Prior research experience

3. Admission to a Graduate Research Program

Applicants must apply for admission into a Master’s by Research or PhD program at Monash University.

4. English Language Proficiency

International applicants must meet the English language requirements set by Monash University.

5. Competitive Selection Process

Scholarships are awarded based on a competitive evaluation process considering:

  • Academic performance

  • Research output

  • Research proposal quality

  • Academic references

Only candidates with exceptional research potential typically receive the award.

Required Documents for Application

Applicants must prepare and submit several documents as part of their scholarship application.

Typical documents include:

  • Academic curriculum vitae (CV)

  • Official academic transcripts and certificates

  • Proof of citizenship or residency

  • English language proficiency test results

  • Detailed research proposal

  • Academic references

  • Invitation to apply (if required by faculty)

All documents must be submitted in English or accompanied by certified translations.

Application Process for the Monash RTP Scholarship 2026

Applying for the RTP Scholarship at Monash University involves a structured application process.

Step 1: Identify a Research Area

Applicants should identify their preferred research area and explore available graduate research programs.

Step 2: Contact a Potential Supervisor

Many faculties recommend contacting a supervisor before submitting the application.

Step 3: Apply for Graduate Research Admission

Applicants must submit an application for admission to a research degree through the Monash online portal.

Step 4: Automatic Scholarship Consideration

Students who apply for graduate research admission are automatically considered for available graduate research scholarships, including the RTP.

Step 5: Wait for Assessment Results

Scholarship decisions are typically announced several weeks after the application deadline.

Apply here

Key Application Deadlines

Monash offers four scholarship rounds each year.

International Applicants

  • Round 1 Deadline: 31st March 2026

  • Round 3 Deadline: 31st August 2026

Domestic Applicants

  • Round 2 Deadline: 31st May 2026

  • Round 4 Deadline: 31st October 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit applications early because the scholarship is highly competitive.

Selection Process

The selection process for the RTP Scholarship is rigorous and merit-based.

Evaluation typically considers:

  • Academic results from previous degrees

  • Research experience

  • Publications and research output

  • Quality of the research proposal

  • Academic referee reports

Because the scholarship is competitive, only candidates with strong academic profiles and significant research potential are selected.

Why Study at Monash University?

Monash University is one of Australia’s leading research institutions and consistently ranks among the world’s top universities.

Key advantages include:

  • Globally recognized research programs

  • Advanced research infrastructure

  • Strong international collaborations

  • Opportunities to work with leading academics

  • Access to global research networks

Studying at Monash also provides students with exposure to a diverse academic environment and opportunities to participate in international research initiatives.

Deadline

International Applicants (Round 1): 31st March 2026

The Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026represents an exceptional opportunity for students seeking fully funded postgraduate research training in Australia. By covering tuition fees, providing a generous stipend, and offering access to world-class research resources, the scholarship allows talented individuals from around the world to pursue advanced academic careers.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Monash Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship 2026 – Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026: Up to Millions in Funding for Private Companies Developing Renewable Energy Projects in Developing Countries
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Norwegian Agency for Development Cooperation (Norad) has launched the Support for Enterprise Development in Renewable Energy in Developing Countries 2026 – Stage 1 (Insights) call for proposals. This initiative is part of a broader effort by the Norwegian government to strengthen private sector participation in renewable energy development across emerging and developing economies. The […]

The post Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026: Up to Millions in Funding for Private Companies Developing Renewable Energy Projects in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Norwegian Agency for Development Cooperation (Norad) has launched the Support for Enterprise Development in Renewable Energy in Developing Countries 2026 – Stage 1 (Insights) call for proposals. This initiative is part of a broader effort by the Norwegian government to strengthen private sector participation in renewable energy development across emerging and developing economies.

The program provides grant funding to commercial enterprises developing renewable energy projects that can increase energy access, stimulate economic growth, and support the global transition to sustainable energy systems. The funding focuses on reducing financial risks during the early stages of project development and preparing projects for investment decisions.

Through this competitive grant scheme, Norad aims to accelerate renewable energy investments in developing countries, particularly in regions where energy infrastructure gaps remain significant.

About the Norad Renewable Energy Enterprise Development Grant

The Norad Enterprise Development Scheme for Renewable Energy is a competitive grant program designed to promote private sector investments in renewable energy projects in developing countries.

The scheme aims to:

  • Increase renewable energy generation capacity.

  • Expand access to reliable electricity in underserved regions.

  • Stimulate sustainable economic development.

  • Reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote climate mitigation.

  • Encourage investment in renewable energy infrastructure.

Many developing countries face major challenges in financing renewable energy projects due to high initial risks, insufficient infrastructure, and limited private sector involvement. This grant addresses those challenges by providing financial support during the project development phase, which is often the most uncertain stage for investors.

Through this initiative, Norad hopes to catalyze investments that will ultimately result in large-scale renewable energy installations and improved energy access.

Objectives of the Grant Program

The primary objective of the Norad Renewable Energy Grant is to support the development of commercially viable renewable energy projects in developing countries.

Specific objectives include:

  1. Supporting early-stage project development.

  2. Reducing financial risks associated with renewable energy investments.

  3. Improving energy infrastructure and grid connectivity.

  4. Encouraging private sector participation in clean energy markets.

  5. Accelerating the global energy transition toward renewable technologies.

By addressing investment barriers and strengthening project preparation activities, the program helps transform innovative ideas into bankable energy projects.

Types of Projects That Can Be Funded

The Norad grant supports several types of activities that help renewable energy projects move toward investment and implementation.

These include:

1. Renewable Energy Project Development Studies

The grant may support feasibility studies and development activities such as:

  • Technical feasibility studies

  • Environmental impact assessments

  • Financial modeling and risk analysis

  • Market and regulatory analysis

  • Project structuring

These activities help determine whether a renewable energy project is technically and financially viable.

2. Infrastructure Enabling Grid Connection

Some renewable energy projects require additional infrastructure to connect to the national electricity grid. The grant may fund activities such as:

  • Access roads to project sites

  • Grid interconnection facilities

  • Transmission or distribution lines necessary for energy evacuation

In cases where infrastructure is funded, ownership of the assets typically transfers to public authorities or utilities.

3. Guarantee Premiums for Debt Financing

The grant may also support the cost of guarantee premiums required for long-term project financing.

This support reduces the risk for lenders and investors, making it easier for developers to secure financing for renewable energy investments.

Funding Amount and Cost Sharing

The Norad Renewable Energy Enterprise Development Grant provides partial financial support.

Key financial conditions include:

  • Grants may cover up to 50 percent of eligible project costs.

  • Applicants must provide at least 50 percent co-financing through their own funds or other financing sources.

  • Payments are made after costs are incurred, meaning applicants must initially cover project expenses themselves before reimbursement.

This co-financing requirement ensures that applicants have strong financial commitment and investment readiness.

Who Can Apply for the Norad Renewable Energy Grant

The program is primarily designed for commercial enterprises operating in the renewable energy sector.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Renewable energy developers

  • Private companies working on renewable power projects

  • Energy investment companies

  • Project development companies

Applications may also be submitted by consortia of eligible companies, provided each partner meets the eligibility criteria.

However, some entities are not eligible to apply directly.

Not Eligible

The following organizations generally cannot apply directly:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

  • Consulting companies without ownership in the project

  • Equipment suppliers

  • Entities without direct involvement in the renewable energy project.

Applicants must demonstrate a significant stake in the project and active involvement in its implementation.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet several technical, financial, and organizational criteria.

Key eligibility requirements include:

1. Legal and Organizational Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be an independent legal commercial entity

  • Provide accurate documentation about their organization

  • Demonstrate ownership or involvement in the project

2. Technical and Operational Capacity

Applicants must show:

  • Technical expertise in renewable energy development

  • Administrative and operational capacity to manage projects

  • Knowledge of the local context and energy sector in the project country

3. Financial Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate financial strength through:

  • Audited financial accounts from previous years

  • Ability to finance at least 50 percent of project costs

  • Capacity to fund activities before reimbursement

In many cases, companies must show minimum turnover from commercial activities to qualify.

4. Governance and Ethical Compliance

Applicants must also demonstrate strong governance frameworks including:

  • Risk management systems

  • Internal control mechanisms

  • Anti-corruption policies

  • Ethical guidelines addressing sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment

  • Compliance with international business standards such as the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights.

Countries Eligible for Project Implementation

Projects must be implemented in developing countries eligible for Norwegian development assistance.

Eligible regions include:

  • Sub-Saharan Africa

  • South Asia

  • Southeast Asia

  • Latin America

  • Middle East and North Africa

  • Small Island Developing States

Examples of eligible countries include:

  • Kenya

  • Nigeria

  • Tanzania

  • Mozambique

  • Nepal

  • Bangladesh

  • Ghana

  • Uganda

  • Pakistan

  • Indonesia

Norad places special priority on Sub-Saharan Africa and Least Developed Countries (LDCs).

Priority countries may include:

  • Malawi

  • Mozambique

  • Nepal

  • Nigeria

  • Somalia

  • Tanzania

    Application Process

    The Norad Renewable Energy Grant uses a two-stage application process.

    Stage 1: Concept Note Submission

    Applicants must first submit:

    • A concept note describing the project

    • Audited financial accounts

    • Confirmation of eligibility criteria

    The concept note provides an overview of the proposed project and allows Norad to assess its potential impact and feasibility.

Stage 2: Full Application

Applicants who pass Stage 1 will be invited to submit a detailed proposal including:

  • Full project documentation

  • Financial models

  • Implementation plans

  • Additional supporting materials

Only shortlisted applicants proceed to this stage.

Apply here

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through Norad’s official grants portal.

Applicants should carefully review:

  • Eligibility requirements

  • Application instructions

  • Required documentation

  • Evaluation criteria

Evaluation and Selection Process

Applications are evaluated based on multiple factors including:

  • Technical feasibility of the project

  • Financial viability

  • Development impact

  • Contribution to renewable energy access

  • Alignment with Norad’s priorities

  • Applicant experience and capacity

Because the program is highly competitive, meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee funding. Norad typically signs 10–20 grant agreements per call, depending on available funding and application quality.

Key Benefits of the Norad Renewable Energy Grant

The grant provides several advantages for renewable energy companies:

1. Reduced Investment Risk – Funding helps reduce financial risk during the early development phase.

2. Increased Access to Project Financing – Support for guarantee premiums improves access to loans and investment capital.

3. Expansion into Emerging Markets – Companies can expand renewable energy projects into high-growth developing markets.

4. Contribution to Climate Goals – Projects contribute to global climate mitigation by increasing renewable energy capacity.

Important Deadline

Applicants must submit their Stage 1 concept note before:

24th March 2026

Late applications will not be accepted.

The Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026 represents a significant opportunity for commercial renewable energy developers and private companies seeking to expand energy projects in developing countries. By providing financial support during early project development, the program helps reduce investment risks, unlock financing opportunities, and accelerate renewable energy deployment in regions where access to electricity remains limited.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Norad Renewable Energy Grant 2026: Up to Millions in Funding for Private Companies Developing Renewable Energy Projects in Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Iris Prize 2026 for Young Environmental Leaders Worldwide (Up to $15,000)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Iris Prize 2026 is an international funding opportunity designed to support young environmental leaders who are actively working to protect and restore nature within their communities. Organized by The Iris Project, this initiative provides financial awards and global recognition to youth-led projects that address environmental challenges and promote sustainable change. The prize exists to […]

The post Iris Prize 2026 for Young Environmental Leaders Worldwide (Up to $15,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Iris Prize 2026 is an international funding opportunity designed to support young environmental leaders who are actively working to protect and restore nature within their communities. Organized by The Iris Project, this initiative provides financial awards and global recognition to youth-led projects that address environmental challenges and promote sustainable change.

The prize exists to empower young people who are leading environmental solutions and to ensure that youth voices are recognized, supported, and funded in the global movement to address the climate and ecological crisis. Young innovators across the world who are developing creative ideas or managing impactful environmental projects can apply for this opportunity to receive funding, mentorship, and access to an international network of environmental leaders.

The Iris Prize specifically aims to address the funding gap faced by youth-led climate initiatives. Despite the growing global youth movement advocating for environmental action, only a small percentage of institutional climate funding reaches youth-led organizations. Through this prize, the organizers aim to change that reality by providing direct support to young environmental changemakers.

Overview of the Iris Prize Categories and Funding Amounts

To ensure that young environmental initiatives at different stages of development receive appropriate support, the Iris Prize program offers three separate award categories. Each category targets projects at a specific stage, from early-stage ideas to established initiatives with the potential to scale globally.

1. Seed Prize

  • Funding Amount: $5,000

  • Target Applicants: Young individuals or teams with innovative environmental ideas.

  • Age Requirement: 14–24 years

The Seed Prize is designed for applicants who have a groundbreaking idea to protect or restore nature but may not yet have fully implemented their project. This category supports early-stage environmental solutions that demonstrate creativity, community impact, and strong potential for future development.

2. Stem Prize

  • Funding Amount: $10,000

  • Target Applicants: Recently established environmental projects operating on a small scale.

  • Age Requirement: 16–24 years

The Stem Prize is intended for youth-led initiatives that have already begun operating and are starting to show measurable results. This category supports projects that need funding to strengthen their activities, expand their impact, or develop further.

3. Iris Prize

  • Funding Amount: $15,000

  • Target Applicants: Established environmental projects with the ability to scale and replicate.

  • Age Requirement: 16–24 years

This is the highest-level award in the program. It is reserved for well-developed youth-led initiatives that are already demonstrating significant environmental impact and have the potential to expand to other communities or regions.

Who Can Apply for the Iris Prize 2026?

The Iris Prize is open to young environmental leaders from across the globe. The program welcomes applications from individuals, teams, or youth-led organizations working on environmental protection or restoration.

Eligible Applicants

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

1. Age Requirement – Applicants must be between 14 and 24 years old depending on the prize category.

2. Youth-Led Projects – The project must be led by young people, meaning that:

  • Most of the leadership team must fall within the eligible age range.

  • Young leaders must have real decision-making authority and autonomy within the project.

3. Environmental Focus – Applicants must be working on projects that support:

  • Protection of natural ecosystems

  • Restoration of environmental resources

  • Advocacy for environmental rights

  • Community-based environmental solutions

4. Geographic Eligibility – Applicants from all countries and continents are eligible to apply. However, the program prioritizes projects from the Global South, where environmental initiatives often face greater funding challenges and where support can have the most impact.

Types of Eligible Projects

The Iris Prize accepts a wide range of environmental initiatives, including:

  • New environmental ideas

  • Small-scale projects

  • Existing environmental programs with growth potential

  • Community-based environmental restoration initiatives

  • Youth environmental advocacy movements

  • Innovative technological solutions addressing ecological challenges

Projects associated with larger organizations, NGOs, or charities may also apply as long as young people maintain full leadership and decision-making control over the project.

Additional Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should also demonstrate the following values and principles:

1. Commitment to Environmental Protection – Applicants must show that their project supports the protection and restoration of nature and promotes environmental sustainability.

2. Community-Based Impact – The project should be locally driven, addressing environmental challenges identified by the community where it operates.

3. Diversity and Inclusion – The program encourages initiatives that promote diversity, inclusion, and equitable participation in environmental action.

4. Safeguarding Requirements for Minors – If applicants are minors according to their country’s laws, they must partner with a local NGO or organization that will act as a fiscal sponsor. This organization will help manage funds and provide financial oversight for the project.

Benefits of the Iris Prize

The Iris Prize offers more than financial funding. Selected applicants gain access to a wide range of professional development opportunities and international networks.

Key benefits include:

1. Financial Support – Successful applicants receive grants ranging from $5,000 to $15,000, depending on the prize category.

2. Global Recognition – Winning projects receive international exposure and recognition for their environmental impact.

3. Networking Opportunities – Applicants are invited to join the Iris Project Ecosystem, a private collaboration platform where participants can connect with other environmental leaders, organizations, and funders.

4. Capacity Building and Mentorship – Participants receive opportunities for training, collaboration, and knowledge-sharing with experts and fellow youth activists.

5. Documentary and Media Coverage – Winning projects may be documented by local filmmakers and featured in the global awards ceremony.

Iris Prize 2026 Application Timeline

The selection process involves several stages designed to identify the most impactful youth-led environmental initiatives.

March 4, 2026

Application portal opens.

March – April 2026

Applicants are invited to join the Iris Project collaboration ecosystem and networking platform.

April 8, 2026

Final application deadline.

May 2026

Shortlisted candidates participate in interviews and due diligence checks.

June 2026

The expert judging panel reviews shortlisted projects.

July 2026

Due diligence and funding verification for selected projects.

August 2026

Winners and runners-up are notified.

September 2026

Official announcement of winners during a Virtual Awards Ceremony.

How to Apply for the Iris Prize 2026

The application process is designed to be accessible to young people from diverse backgrounds.

Step 1: Complete the Eligibility Quiz

Applicants must first complete an eligibility quiz available on the official application page.

Step 2: Access the Application Form

Once eligibility is confirmed, applicants gain access to the full application form.

Step 3: Submit Project Details

Applicants must provide information about their project, including:

  • The environmental problem being addressed

  • The proposed solution

  • The role of young people in the project

  • How the initiative promotes diversity and inclusion

Step 4: Submit the Application

Applications can be submitted online or offline and are accepted in any language, making the opportunity accessible to young leaders worldwide.

Apply here

Languages Accepted for the Application

The Iris Prize application materials are available in several languages, including:

  • English

  • Spanish

  • Portuguese

  • French

  • Arabic

This multilingual approach ensures that youth from different linguistic backgrounds can apply without language barriers.

Why Young Environmental Leaders Should Apply

The Iris Prize represents one of the few global funding opportunities designed specifically for youth-led environmental initiatives. With climate change and environmental degradation posing urgent global challenges, empowering young innovators has become a crucial part of developing sustainable solutions.

By supporting youth leadership, the Iris Prize encourages innovative environmental thinking, strengthens grassroots environmental movements, and amplifies the voices of young changemakers worldwide.

Application Deadline

8th April 2026 (23:59 CET)

The Iris Prize 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young environmental leaders to transform their ideas into impactful initiatives. Through financial grants, mentorship, and global exposure, the program helps young innovators expand their environmental solutions and inspire change within their communities.

If you are a young environmental activist, innovator, or community leader with a passion for protecting nature, the Iris Prize could provide the funding and support needed to scale your impact.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Iris Prize 2026 for Young Environmental Leaders Worldwide (Up to $15,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 in Nepal – 100% Scholarship Opportunity Available for ASEAN and SAARC Citizens
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026, an immersive eight-day professional development program designed to equip participants with essential facilitation, leadership, and outdoor education skills. The training will take place in a nature-rich environment near Kathmandu, Nepal, offering participants a unique opportunity to explore how learning, leadership, and […]

The post Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 in Nepal – 100% Scholarship Opportunity Available for ASEAN and SAARC Citizens appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026, an immersive eight-day professional development program designed to equip participants with essential facilitation, leadership, and outdoor education skills. The training will take place in a nature-rich environment near Kathmandu, Nepal, offering participants a unique opportunity to explore how learning, leadership, and personal development can unfold through nature-based experiences.

Organized by YEP Academy, this program is designed for individuals who aspire to facilitate meaningful learning processes in outdoor environments. Participants will learn how to guide groups through experiential learning, design impactful outdoor educational programs, and build the core competencies required to lead groups safely and ethically in nature-based settings.

In addition to professional training, the program offers 100% scholarships supported through donor subsidies from the German Government, specifically available for citizens of ASEAN and SAARC countries.

Program Overview

Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature serves as a foundational training for individuals who want to develop skills in outdoor facilitation and experiential education. The program combines theory, practical training, and reflective learning processes to help participants become confident facilitators capable of guiding individuals and groups through meaningful learning experiences in natural environments.

The program focuses on helping participants understand how people learn individually and in groups, how facilitators can support these processes, and how outdoor environments can be used as powerful learning spaces.

Through experiential learning and reflective practice, participants will build confidence in their ability to design and facilitate safe, ethical, and impactful outdoor learning journeys.

Program Dates and Location

The training will take place in Nepal in a nature-rich setting near Kathmandu using a basecamp model that allows participants to immerse themselves fully in the outdoor learning experience.

Participants will meet at the training location on 19 April 2026 at 2:00 PM, giving everyone time to settle in and prepare their tents and equipment before the program officially begins at 5:00 PM.

The course will run for eight days and seven nights, concluding on 26 April 2026 around 2:00 PM.

Program Structure and Learning Experience

The training is structured as an immersive residential experience that blends facilitation theory, outdoor skills, group work, and reflective practice. Each day follows a rhythm that allows participants to experience learning processes directly while also reflecting on them from the perspective of a facilitator.

Typical daily activities include morning check-ins that help participants connect with themselves and the group while setting intentions for the day. Nature-based experiential learning sessions form a central component of the program, enabling participants to explore leadership, communication, and group dynamics through direct engagement with the natural environment.

Facilitation input sessions provide theoretical frameworks, tools, and models that help participants understand how learning processes unfold and how facilitators can guide them effectively. Participants also engage in guided group work and practice sessions where they apply facilitation skills and communication techniques in real learning scenarios.

Reflection and feedback sessions help deepen understanding and allow participants to practice giving and receiving constructive feedback. Evening circles and storytelling sessions provide space for participants to reflect on experiences, process emotions, and strengthen trust within the group.

The program’s flexible structure adapts to weather conditions, the surrounding environment, and the developmental stages of the group, allowing learning to emerge organically while simulating real conditions faced by outdoor facilitators.

Key Learning Topics

Participants will explore several important topics related to facilitation, leadership, and experiential learning.

The program covers how people learn across different life stages, including children, adolescents, and adults, and what these learning processes mean for outdoor education. Participants will also examine the ethical responsibilities and roles of facilitators, distinguishing between facilitation, training, and coaching.

Core facilitation skills are emphasized, including session structure, guiding group processes, asking powerful questions, and creating spaces where participants feel safe and supported.

Communication training includes active listening, nonviolent communication, feedback culture, and conflict awareness. Participants will learn how to recognize and respond to emotional dynamics within groups while maintaining respectful and constructive communication.

Group development processes are explored to help participants understand how groups evolve and how facilitation approaches must adapt accordingly. The program also introduces basic conflict awareness and de-escalation techniques.

Participants will develop awareness of outdoor safety principles such as risk perception, safety mindset, and basic field routines that help ensure safe group experiences in nature.

The training also emphasizes facilitator self-care and self-management practices, including grounding techniques, emotional resilience, and maintaining personal boundaries when leading groups.

Outdoor Skills and Facilitation Competencies

Participants will develop practical outdoor facilitation competencies, including camp management, risk awareness, and first aid considerations in outdoor environments. They will learn how to design and implement outdoor learning programs, prepare facilitation agendas, and co-facilitate learning sessions effectively.

The program also helps participants develop the confidence to navigate group dynamics, guide reflective learning processes, and evaluate educational experiences with professionalism and presence.

Facilitators

The program is led by experienced practitioners in outdoor education and facilitation.

The lead facilitator is Dirk Reber, a practitioner specializing in ecotherapy, outdoor education, and process facilitation. He has decades of experience designing and leading transformative nature-based learning programs across diverse ecological and cultural contexts. He oversees curriculum design, program leadership, safety coordination, supervision, and participant feedback. Full bio and links will be available on the Team Page.

The co-facilitator is Anu Rai, a seasoned trekker and educator with expertise in transforming outdoor adventures into transformative learning experiences. Her work focuses on sustainable trekking, environmental awareness, and experiential education in mountain and forest environments. Full bio and links will be available on the Team Page.

Participant Group Size

To ensure a safe and meaningful learning environment, the program maintains a small participant group size. A minimum of six and a maximum of twelve participants will be accepted for the training.

This small cohort allows facilitators to provide individualized support, foster deeper interaction among participants, and create a strong sense of community throughout the learning journey.

Certification

Participants who successfully complete the program will receive a Certificate of Continuing Professional Development (CPD).

To qualify for certification, participants must attend all sessions and actively engage in group processes, reflective activities, and facilitation practice.

Participants who wish to pursue further professional certification can continue their training through additional programs offered by YEP Academy. Completion of further intensive training journeys and a mentored facilitation practice phase may lead to certification as a Certified Outdoor Youth Facilitator.

Participants who complete the full training pathway and develop their own outdoor learning program may receive the designation International Nature and Outdoor Youth Facilitator and Leader.

Safety and Risk Management

Participant safety and well-being are central to the program design. Comprehensive risk management procedures are implemented to ensure a safe learning environment.

Participants provide emergency contact and medical information during registration. Signed consent forms confirm understanding of safety procedures before the program begins.

All facilitators are certified in first aid, and accident insurance coverage of up to USD 5,000 is provided for each participant.

Location-specific risk assessments are conducted prior to the program to identify and mitigate potential hazards. The campsite environment is designed to provide a stable and secure basecamp where participants can focus on learning without exposure to high-risk adventure activities.

Program Fee and What Is Included

The program fee covers all facilitation sessions and training materials provided throughout the program.

Accommodation and campsite fees are included as part of the basecamp learning model. Accident insurance coverage is also included.

Food arrangements include daily lunches prepared and served during the program, as well as group-prepared dinners where ingredients are provided and participants cook together. Breakfast is organized independently by participants.

Participants will also attend a preparation meeting held online two weeks prior to the program.

What Is Not Included

Participants are responsible for arranging travel to and from the meeting location in Nepal.

Visa fees, vaccinations, and personal outdoor equipment such as tents, sleeping bags, clothing, and daypacks are not included in the program fee.

Accommodation before or after the training is also not included unless otherwise specified.

Optional equipment rental may be available through YEP Academy for tents, sleeping bags, and sleeping mats for an additional fee.

Scholarships

The program offers 100 percent scholarships funded through donor subsidies from the German Government.

These scholarships are available exclusively for citizens of ASEAN and SAARC countries.

Applicants who wish to apply for the scholarship must download the scholarship application form and submit it by email to info@yep-academy.org.

Scholarship Deadline

The deadline to apply for the scholarship is 5 April 2026.

Standard Ticket Registration

Participants who do not apply for the scholarship may register for the program through the standard ticket option.

The standard ticket costs USD 890, and ticket sales close on 14 April 2026 at 11:50 PM.

Where can I find important downloads?

  • Download to Know More What to Pack: Packing List

  • Download to Get to Know the Dos and Don’ts: Code of Conduct

  • Download to Be Aware of Our Policies: Terms and Conditions

Contact Information

For further information about the program, applicants may contact YEP Academy via email at info@yep-academy.org.

Read more and apply for the Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 HERE.

Click HERE to explore more global opportunities on OFY.

The post Outdoor Facilitation: The Art of Facilitation in Nature 2026 in Nepal – 100% Scholarship Opportunity Available for ASEAN and SAARC Citizens appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO 2026 Global Opportunity: Apply for the “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement” Programme (Fully Supported Policy Development Initiative
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL), in collaboration with Shanghai Open University, has announced a new international programme aimed at strengthening lifelong learning systems worldwide. This programme forms part of UNESCO’s broader commitment to supporting governments in developing inclusive, flexible and sustainable learning systems that provide education opportunities throughout a person’s life. By encouraging […]

The post UNESCO 2026 Global Opportunity: Apply for the “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement” Programme (Fully Supported Policy Development Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL), in collaboration with Shanghai Open University, has announced a new international programme aimed at strengthening lifelong learning systems worldwide. This programme forms part of UNESCO’s broader commitment to supporting governments in developing inclusive, flexible and sustainable learning systems that provide education opportunities throughout a person’s life. By encouraging cooperation across multiple sectors and levels of governance, the initiative aims to create stronger learning ecosystems capable of responding to the social, economic and technological challenges of the 21st century.

Introduction

The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) has officially announced a global call for applications for its 2026 programme titled “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement.” This initiative is implemented in partnership with Shanghai Open University (SOU) and is designed to support countries in building stronger and more integrated lifelong learning systems.

The programme represents the first phase of a three-year international initiative aimed at strengthening national education systems by integrating lifelong learning into governance, policy frameworks, and institutional coordination across sectors. Participating countries will receive technical support, policy guidance, and international collaboration opportunities to advance their lifelong learning strategies and improve education systems at national and local levels.

This opportunity is particularly important in the context of global education reforms aligned with Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4), which calls for inclusive, equitable quality education and lifelong learning opportunities for all.

Overview of the UNESCO Lifelong Learning Systems Programme

The Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement programme focuses on helping countries design, implement, and strengthen policies that support learning throughout life.

The programme recognizes that education systems must go beyond traditional schooling to address the needs of adults, workers, communities, and lifelong learners. By fostering collaboration across governments, institutions, and sectors, the initiative aims to create coherent learning ecosystems that support individuals throughout their lives.

Countries selected for the 2026 cohort will participate in a structured policy development and capacity-building process supported by global experts in education governance, lifelong learning, and policy implementation.

Objectives of the Programme

The programme seeks to help participating countries achieve several strategic objectives related to lifelong learning systems.

1. Strengthening Governance and Policy Coordination

One of the main objectives of the programme is to improve the governance structures that oversee lifelong learning policies. Many countries have fragmented education policies that separate adult education, vocational training, and formal schooling. The programme helps governments integrate these components into a coherent lifelong learning system.

2. Improving Alignment Between National and Local Policies

Countries often face challenges in translating national education policies into effective local implementation. Through this programme, countries will work to ensure that national strategies align with implementation at regional and city levels, including learning cities and local education ecosystems.

3. Developing Policy Instruments for Lifelong Learning

Another major objective is to help governments develop or refine policy tools such as:

  • National lifelong learning strategies

  • Action plans for adult education

  • Education reform roadmaps

  • Skills development frameworks

These policy instruments help countries create sustainable structures for learning across all stages of life.

4. Building National Capacity

Participating countries will receive support to build national capacity for managing lifelong learning as a cross-sectoral policy area involving education, labor markets, community institutions, and civil society.

Benefits of the Programme

Countries selected for this initiative will gain access to a wide range of benefits designed to support policy reform and knowledge exchange.

1. Tailored Technical Assistance

Participating countries will receive direct technical support from experts affiliated with the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning and partner institutions. This assistance will help countries identify policy gaps and implement effective reforms.

2. Online Learning Modules and Webinars

Participants will take part in curated online training modules and international webinars focusing on:

  • Lifelong learning systems

  • Education governance

  • policy implementation

  • cross-sector collaboration

  • capacity building

These sessions allow national stakeholders to develop new expertise in designing lifelong learning frameworks.

3. National Lifelong Learning System Profile

Each participating country will conduct a contextual analysis of its current lifelong learning ecosystem. The analysis will lead to the creation of a national profile describing:

  • policy frameworks

  • institutional arrangements

  • education infrastructure

  • governance structures

  • future reform priorities

4. Participation in the GNLC Academy Campus

Countries may also participate in activities connected to the Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC). This engagement allows policymakers to collaborate with cities and governments worldwide that are actively developing lifelong learning ecosystems.

5. Policy Dialogue Events

Selected countries will receive support to organize a national or sub-regional policy dialogue event during 2026. These dialogues bring together stakeholders such as:

  • government ministries

  • researchers

  • education institutions

  • civil society organizations

  • development partners

The discussions help translate programme outcomes into concrete policy changes.

6. Follow-Up Support

After policy dialogue events, UNESCO experts will provide follow-up assistance to help integrate the results into national policies and reforms.

Category of Applicants Eligible for the Programme

Unlike individual scholarships or fellowships, this programme is designed for national government institutions.

Eligible Applicants

Applications must be submitted by:

  • National ministries responsible for lifelong learning

  • Ministries of education or equivalent government bodies

  • National agencies responsible for adult education or education policy

Applications must be coordinated with relevant national stakeholders, including local governments and education institutions.

Countries Eligible to Apply

The programme is open to UNESCO Member States worldwide.

This means that countries from all regions and continents may apply, including:

  • Africa

  • Asia

  • Europe

  • North America

  • South America

  • Oceania

  • Middle East

The programme seeks to include a small and diverse cohort of countries to ensure meaningful collaboration and peer learning.

Key Requirements for Participating Countries

Countries selected for the programme must demonstrate strong institutional commitment.

1. Official Government Engagement

Applications must be submitted by a national ministry or relevant government authority responsible for lifelong learning policy.

2. National Leadership Team

Each participating country must designate a national leadership team that will coordinate programme activities.

3. Stakeholder Coalition

Countries must involve stakeholders from different sectors, including:

• education authorities

• local governments

• civil society organizations

• learning cities

• research institutions

4. Resource Mobilization

Participating countries must mobilize the necessary human and financial resources required to implement programme activities.

5. Policy Dialogue Organization

Each country must organize at least one national or sub-regional policy dialogue event during the programme.

6. Policy Integration

Countries are expected to integrate programme results into public policies and national education strategies.

Programme Timeline

The initiative begins in 2026 and forms part of a three-year policy development programme.

Key stages include:

  1. Selection of participating countries

  2. Orientation and programme onboarding

  3. Online learning modules and webinars

  4. National contextual analysis

  5. Policy dialogue events

  6. Follow-up policy implementation support

Why Lifelong Learning Systems Matter

Education is increasingly recognized as a lifelong process that continues beyond traditional schooling. Rapid technological change, evolving labor markets, and global challenges such as climate change require individuals to continuously update their knowledge and skills.

Lifelong learning policies support:

  • adult education and literacy

  • workforce reskilling and upskilling

  • social inclusion

  • community development

  • sustainable economic growth

International organizations such as UNESCO view lifelong learning systems as a critical foundation for inclusive and sustainable societies.

Application Process

Interested governments must complete the official application form provided by the UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning. Apply here

Applications should outline:

  • national priorities for lifelong learning

  • current policy frameworks

  • stakeholders involved

  • commitment to programme participation

After selection, participating countries will formalize their involvement through a letter of intent issued by the responsible ministry.

Application Deadline

20th March 2026

The UNESCO programme on Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement offers a unique opportunity for governments to improve their education policies and develop integrated lifelong learning frameworks.

Through international collaboration, technical expertise, and policy dialogue, participating countries will gain the tools needed to build inclusive, flexible, and resilient education systems capable of supporting learning throughout life.

For countries committed to advancing education reform and achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4, this initiative represents a strategic opportunity to strengthen national education ecosystems and create sustainable lifelong learning pathways for their citizens.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post UNESCO 2026 Global Opportunity: Apply for the “Strengthening Lifelong Learning Systems through Multi-Level and Multi-Sectoral Engagement” Programme (Fully Supported Policy Development Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Research Funding: €12M Horizon Europe Call on Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Researchers and institutions working on sustainable agriculture now have an exciting opportunity to secure major European research funding. The European Commission has launched a new call under Horizon Europe Cluster 6, inviting proposals that focus on innovative solutions for sustainable and climate-smart agriculture systems. The call—HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-FARM2FORK-02 (two-stage)—supports research projects aimed at optimising the water-nutrient-energy nexus […]

The post Global Research Funding: €12M Horizon Europe Call on Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers and institutions working on sustainable agriculture now have an exciting opportunity to secure major European research funding. The European Commission has launched a new call under Horizon Europe Cluster 6, inviting proposals that focus on innovative solutions for sustainable and climate-smart agriculture systems.

The call—HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-FARM2FORK-02 (two-stage)—supports research projects aimed at optimising the water-nutrient-energy nexus to strengthen agricultural sustainability and improve food system resilience. The initiative aligns with the European Union Farm‑to‑Fork Strategy, which seeks to transform food systems to become fair, healthy, and environmentally friendly.

About the Funding Opportunity

This Horizon Europe call will fund collaborative research projects that advance sustainable food production systems while improving resource efficiency across agriculture. Projects are expected to generate innovative technologies, strategies, or practices that can strengthen climate-smart agriculture and enhance food security.

Successful proposals should demonstrate strong scientific excellence, innovation potential, and clear impact on sustainable and resilient food systems.

Funding Details

  • Total indicative budget: €12,000,000

  • Estimated EU contribution per project: Around €6,000,000

  • Funding type: Horizon Europe Innovation Action (Lump-Sum Grant)

  • Call reference: HORIZON-CL6-2027-02-FARM2FORK-02 (two-stage)

Focus Areas

Projects funded under this call are expected to:

  • Develop innovative solutions that optimise the water–nutrient–energy nexus in agriculture

  • Support climate-smart and sustainable agricultural systems

  • Improve resource efficiency in food production

  • Contribute to resilient and sustainable food systems aligned with the EU Farm-to-Fork strategy

  • Strengthen research and innovation that enhances the sustainability and competitiveness of agricultural systems

Eligibility Criteria

The call is open to a wide range of stakeholders including:

  • Research institutions

  • Universities

  • Private companies

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

  • Multi-stakeholder research consortia

Applicants must submit proposals as international research consortia, typically involving organizations from at least three different EU Member States or Horizon Europe Associated Countries.

Geographic Scope

The call primarily targets organizations based in:

  • European Union Member States

  • Horizon Europe Associated Countries

However, international partners—including organizations from Africa and other regions—can participate as members of eligible research consortia, making this a valuable opportunity for African research institutions seeking international collaboration.

Important Dates

  • Call opening date: 3 February 2027

  • First-stage proposal deadline: 7 April 2027

How to Apply

Interested applicants can access the application portal and detailed call information here:

Apply Here

Stay Updated

To receive verified updates on funding opportunities, partnerships, fellowships, and global programs for Africans, follow Global Grants and Opportunities for Africa (GGA):

Discover more Grant opportunities Here.

The post Global Research Funding: €12M Horizon Europe Call on Sustainable Agriculture and Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Language learning is no longer limited to classrooms or textbooks. With the increasing importance of global communication and cultural understanding, innovative programs are emerging to connect students worldwide. One such initiative is TOCA Online, a virtual language learning and cultural exchange program that provides students with the opportunity to build language skills while connecting with […]

The post TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Language learning is no longer limited to classrooms or textbooks. With the increasing importance of global communication and cultural understanding, innovative programs are emerging to connect students worldwide. One such initiative is TOCA Online, a virtual language learning and cultural exchange program that provides students with the opportunity to build language skills while connecting with diverse communities across the globe.

Implemented by Teens of Color Abroad (TOCA) and supported by the J. Christopher Stevens Virtual Exchange Initiative (JCSVEI) under the U.S. Department of State’s Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs, TOCA Online aims to create meaningful international connections through virtual exchange.

The program is designed for high school and university students who want to improve their language proficiency, develop intercultural communication skills, and engage in global conversations with peers and refugee educators worldwide.

What is TOCA Online?

TOCA Online is a virtual language and cultural exchange program that allows students to learn languages such as Arabic, French, and Spanish from native speakers. The program places a strong emphasis on dialogue, cultural awareness, and global collaboration.

Students are taught by refugee language partners who are native speakers of the language they teach. These instructors are located in different parts of the world and bring valuable cultural perspectives into each lesson. Through this approach, students gain both linguistic competence and cultural understanding.

The program also integrates workshops, peer exchanges, and collaborative projects designed to encourage cross-cultural dialogue and global awareness among participants.

Program Structure

TOCA Online offers two main program tracks:

High School Virtual Language Program

The high school program is designed for students between the ages of 13 and 18 and runs for three weeks. During this period, students participate in virtual language instruction sessions combined with cultural exchange activities.

Participants can choose from several language options depending on their interests and goals.

Available courses include:

  • Introduction to Arabic (20 hours)

  • Introduction to French (10 hours)

  • Introduction to Spanish (10 hours)

  • Advanced Spanish (10 hours)

Students take part in live online sessions where they practice speaking and listening skills while interacting with peers from around the world.

In addition to language classes, students attend Refugee Voices workshops, which feature distinguished refugee speakers who share their stories and experiences. These sessions provide students with insights into global migration, resilience, and cultural diversity.

College-Level Arabic Language Exchange Program

TOCA Online also offers a college-level program focused on Arabic language practice and cross-cultural collaboration.

This program connects students from Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in the United States with university students from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

Participants engage in:

  • One-on-one Arabic and English language exchanges

  • Interactive online modules

  • Cultural workshops

  • Collaborative virtual sessions

The exchange typically runs for seven to eight weeks, allowing participants to build meaningful connections while improving their language skills.

At the end of the program, American participants submit short video introductions in Arabic demonstrating their newly acquired skills, while MENA participants present their experiences collaborating with U.S. students and discuss how the program helped prepare them for the workforce.

Currently, the college-level opportunity is available only to students from partnering HBCUs.

Program Options and Costs

TOCA Online provides two participation options for high school students.

Standard Program

The standard program is offered free of charge, making it accessible to students from various backgrounds.

Participants in the standard program receive:

  • Live virtual language instruction

  • Refugee Voices workshops

  • Cultural exchange sessions

  • A digital certificate of completion

  • Access to alumni opportunities and networking resources

Immersive Program

Students seeking a more intensive experience can enroll in the Immersive Program, which costs USD 250.

This option includes all benefits of the standard program plus additional features such as:

  • Five hours of personalized one-on-one language practice

  • A TOCA completion package containing a t-shirt, pen, sticker, printed certificate, and activity book

  • Invitations to three virtual culinary lessons

The immersive option allows participants to deepen their language practice and gain a richer cultural experience.

Key Benefits of Participating in TOCA Online

Participating in TOCA Online offers several valuable benefits for students interested in international education and cross-cultural communication.

Development of Language Skills

Students significantly improve their speaking and listening skills by interacting directly with native speakers through live sessions.

Cultural Exchange and Global Awareness

The program fosters meaningful cultural exchange by connecting students with peers and educators from diverse backgrounds worldwide.

Networking and Global Connections

Participants build friendships and professional connections with other globally minded students and educators.

Access to Future Opportunities

Students receive access to scholarships, educational opportunities, and international programs through TOCA’s alumni network and newsletters.

Certificates and Recommendations

Participants earn a certificate of completion and may qualify for letters of recommendation to support future academic and career pursuits.

Why Virtual Exchange Matters

Virtual exchange programs like TOCA Online are becoming increasingly important in the modern educational landscape. They allow students to connect with people across borders, develop intercultural competence, and gain real-world communication skills.

These experiences prepare young people for global careers in fields such as international relations, diplomacy, global business, education, and humanitarian work.

By engaging with students and educators worldwide, participants develop empathy, cultural understanding, and the ability to collaborate across cultural and geographic boundaries.

Testimonials from Participants

Students, educators, and parents consistently praise the impact of the TOCA Online program.

One educator noted that the program complements classroom learning by reinforcing language skills in an interactive environment.

Parents have also highlighted the program’s impact on their children’s academic and career paths. Some participants have gone on to pursue language studies at the university level after completing the program.

Many students report gaining confidence in speaking their target language and developing meaningful international friendships through the program.

Application Timeline

The application cycle for the 2025 TOCA Online program has closed. Students interested in participating in the future are encouraged to join the program’s newsletter to receive updates when applications open for the 2026 season.

Applicants may be invited to participate in a virtual interview with TOCA staff to discuss their interest and commitment to the program.

Prospective participants are encouraged to monitor announcements to ensure they do not miss the next application cycle.

for more information click here

Discover more opportunities here  

The post TOCA Online Virtual Language Fellowship: Study Arabic, French, or Spanish with Global Peers Through Cultural Exchange appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
